data_in.description: D-Pantothenic Acid MS/MS spectrum from MassBank accession MSBNK-RIKEN-PR100295

data_out.description: CSV library entry containing annotated fragments with mass-to-charge, intensity, and fragment match scores

domain_knowledge: Noise filtering at 0.005 intensity units removes low-abundance background signals; marker peak filtering at 0.1 selects fragment ions with significant abundance for reliable matching.
Mass tolerance (mzTol=0.01) defines the allowable ppm or Da window for fragment peak matching against reference databases; stricter tolerance reduces false-positive annotations.
The mpeaksScore parameter (0.9) sets the minimum cosine similarity or dot-product score required to accept a fragment-ion match, controlling specificity of library entry assignments.
D-Pantothenic Acid [M+H]+ generates characteristic fragment ions that serve as a positive-control case for validating the genFragEntry workflow on a known metabolite.

evidence_snippets.text: [other] How does the genFragEntry function process an experimental MS/MS spectrum to generate a metabolite library entry by applying peak-picking thresholds and occurrence scoring?: 'This function will attribute occurrence scores to the peaks above *mpeaksThres* threshold ("marker peaks") and above the noise level.'

[other] genFragEntry converts an MS/MS spectrum into a library entry by identifying marker peaks above the mpeaksThres threshold (0.1 default) and noise level (0.005 default), attributing occurrence scores to these peaks, with parameters including mpeaksScore=0.9, mzTol=0.01, and requires explicit definition of metabolite name, adduct notation, accurate adduct m/z, and output filename.: 'This function will attribute occurrence scores to the peaks above *mpeaksThres* threshold ("marker peaks") and above the noise level. cmd: library(MetaboAnnotatoR); spectrum_data <- read.csv('{inputs.massbank_spectrum}'); result <- genFragEntry(spectrum_data, noise=0.005, mpeaksScore=0.9, mpeaksThres=0.1, mzTol=0.01); write.csv(result, 'library_entry_{task_id}.csv', row.names=FALSE)

expected_outputs: CSV library entry containing annotated fragments with mass-to-charge, intensity, and fragment match scores
finding: genFragEntry converts an MS/MS spectrum into a library entry by identifying marker peaks above the mpeaksThres threshold (0.1 default) and noise level (0.005 default), attributing occurrence scores to these peaks, with parameters including mpeaksScore=0.9, mzTol=0.01, and requires explicit definition of metabolite name, adduct notation, accurate adduct m/z, and output filename.
inputs: D-Pantothenic Acid MS/MS spectrum from MassBank accession MSBNK-RIKEN-PR100295
methodology_summary: Load MS/MS spectrum data from MassBank for D-Pantothenic Acid
Apply noise threshold filtering at intensity ≥0.005 to remove background signals
Apply marker peak threshold filtering at intensity ≥0.1 to identify significant fragments
Execute genFragEntry with mpeaksScore=0.9 and mzTol=0.01 to score and annotate fragment matches
Validation: Output CSV library entry contains annotated fragment ions with mass, intensity, and match scores; structure matches MetaboAnnotatoR library entry format

task_objective: Convert the D-Pantothenic Acid MS/MS spectrum using genFragEntry with specified parameters (noise=0.005, mpeaksScore=0.9, mpeaksThres=0.1, mzTol=0.01) to produce a CSV library entry file.

Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: CSV library entry containing annotated fragments with mass-to-charge, intensity, and fragment match scores.
workflow_description: 1. Load the D-Pantothenic Acid MS/MS spectrum from MassBank accession MSBNK-RIKEN-PR100295. 2. Apply peak-picking filtering at noise threshold of 0.005 to remove low-intensity signals. 3. Apply marker peak filtering at threshold of 0.1 to identify significant fragment peaks. 4. Execute genFragEntry function with mpeaksScore=0.9 and mzTol=0.01 to annotate fragment ions and generate scored matches. 5. Output results as a CSV-formatted library entry containing annotated fragments with mass, intensity, and match scores.
